registration.proposal_source: idea-engine control/outbox.md @ main b77beca (landed via idea-engine PR #358, merged 2026-07-13T22:30:14Z)
registration.verdict: VERDICT 060 (INTAKE 049; INTAKE number = proposal number per the established offset map)

harvest_pin.note: citation only — the sim is fully hermetic: zero repo/network reads at verdict time; every constant below is pinned in the idea file and copied verbatim from it

lines_56_59: magnet_fit    = 0.15;   // how much SMALLER the pocket is than the magnet (mm).
// 0.10-0.20 = a firm press-fit on most printers. Won't go
// in? Raise it (e.g. 0.25) or sand the magnet. Drops out?
// Lower it, or add a drop of glue.
magnet_tool_scad.bench_words_lines_40_41: press-fit : a pocket a hair SMALLER than the magnet so it wedges in and stays with no glue. Loose? A drop of glue fixes it.
magnet_tool_scad.header_lines_22_24_fragment: authored with no OpenSCAD installed, so this has NOT been rendered or sliced

effector_mount_readme.lines_113_117_fragment: The pocket prints this much smaller than the magnet so it wedges in. If your printer runs tight and the magnet won't seat, raise it; if the magnet drops out, lower it (or add a drop of glue)
effector_mount_readme.inline_echo: magnet_fit = 0.15;   // <- 0.10–0.20 firm press-fit; raise if too tight

lines_26_28: clear_min  = 0.10;   // smallest radial clearance to test (mm, per side)
clear_max  = 0.50;   // largest  radial clearance to test (mm, per side)
tolerance_test_coin_scad.lines_14_16_unit_rule_fragment: It is measured PER SIDE here: a hole labelled 0.20 has 0.20 mm of gap on every side, so the two sides add up to 0.40 mm across the diameter -- the classic gotcha

lines_10_12_bands: - **Press** — tightest gap that pushed in firmly and held (no wobble).
- **Snug** — gap that slid in snug with no side-to-side play.
- **Loose** — gap where the pin dropped in and rattled.
clearance_results_md.line_36_exemplar_row: | Generic grey PLA | 2026-07-13 | 0.10 | 0.20 | 0.45 | 210 °C, 0.2 mm layer, elephant-foot 0.2 |

model.units: all lengths INTEGER HUNDREDTHS of a millimetre (cmm), all DIAMETRAL (the coin's per-side values doubled on entry per its own lines 14-16); all probabilities exact fractions.Fraction on the equiprobable integer lattice

hole_share_rho.note: a CHOICE, disclosed — the coin measures pin + hole combined; the H sensitivity pair brackets it

H.note: systematic hole undersize, uniform integer, cmm diametral; center = exemplar's 20; span INVENTED, pinned, disclosed

eta.note: per-print noise, uniform integer, cmm diametral; INVENTED, pinned, disclosed

m.note: magnet diameter error, uniform integer, cmm diametral; INVENTED, pinned, disclosed

population.independence: H, m, eta independent (named in the idea doc's Model basis)

model.metrics: per F: DROP(F), UNSEAT(F), FAIL(F) = DROP(F) + UNSEAT(F) as exact Fractions over the 9,471-cell equiprobable lattice; plus reporting-only FAIL_glue(F) = UNSEAT(F) and the per-H conditional FAIL(15 | H) curve

decision_rule.REJECT: checked FIRST: iff FAIL(15) > 1/10 (the shipped universal default is dishonest across the pinned population; the honest instruction is the repo's own calibrate-first coin doctrine)
decision_rule.INVALID: iff the seed-20261334 zero-noise identity fails in either arm, OR a monotonicity theorem gate fails (DROP(F) non-increasing and UNSEAT(F) non-decreasing in F), OR Arm B breaches the agreement gate on any grid cell — report, do not rule
decision_rule.APPROVE: iff FAIL(15) <= 1/20 AND FAIL(15) <= min_F FAIL(F) + 1/100 AND the seed-20261336 stability leg reproduces the ruling through the twin evaluators
decision_rule.NULL: anything else — four pre-registered axes: (i) band-miss FAIL(15) in (1/20, 1/10]; (ii) wrong-center — FAIL(15) <= 1/20 but some grid F beats it by more than 1/100; (iii) stability non-reproduction; (iv) sensitivity straddle — a reporting-only world lands a primary conjunct on the other side of a band edge (named axis is the finding; reporting legs never flip the decision)

arm_A.role: DECISION — seedless exact full enumeration of the 9,471-cell lattice per grid F; every probability an exact Fraction; run twice in-process and identity-checked; byte-identical across process runs; the ruling rides Arm A alone

arm_B.role: VALIDATION only — never overrides Arm A
arm_B.draw_order: per scenario, pinned: H then m then eta (one randint call each on the counting RNG wrapper)
arm_B.common_random_numbers: one scenario set of N draws via random.Random(20261333), evaluated at all six grid F
arm_B.agreement_gate: |FAILhat(F) - FAIL(F)| <= 1/100 absolute AND <= 4*se on every grid cell, se = sqrt(pA*(1-pA)/N) with pA = Arm A's exact FAIL(F); any breach is a control failure (INVALID), never overridden

seeds.20261333: Arm B headline: N = 200,000 common-random-numbers validation across all six F (draw sentinel 600,000 randint calls)
seeds.20261334: Arm B control: zero-noise identity world (eta ≡ 0, m ≡ 0, H ≡ 20) — I = F + 20 in [25, 50] for every grid F, so every cell must return PRESS with probability EXACTLY 1 in both arms (exact identity, not a tolerance); any other value is a misbehaving control → INVALID; N = 20,000 (fixture-choice, disclosed — the registration pins the world, not the control N; draw sentinel 60,000)
seeds.20261335: Arm B sensitivity confirmations: MC re-runs of the ten reporting-only sensitivity worlds, N = 20,000 each, one RNG in the pinned world order (draw sentinel 600,000); reporting-only, can never flip the decision
seeds.20261336: stability leg: re-run the headline at N = 20,000 (draw sentinel 60,000); PASS iff the ruling class reproduces through the twin independently-written evaluators
seeds.policy: exactly these four, pre-registered in PROPOSAL 049, strictly above the P048/V059 high-water 20261332; no other RNG constructed; no aux reads

sensitivity_worlds.note: seed 20261335, N = 20,000 each, REPORTING-ONLY — each world changes exactly ONE component from the headline pin; Arm A exact enumeration per world plus the MC confirmation; pinned order below

reporting_only.full_table: DROP/UNSEAT/FAIL × F with the DROP-vs-UNSEAT split per cell (the glue-asymmetry exhibit)
reporting_only.glue_fallback: FAIL_glue(F) = UNSEAT(F) — the grid re-read under the repo's own one-drop-of-glue remedy
reporting_only.per_H_conditional: FAIL(15 | H) for H in 0..40 over the 11 × 21 sub-lattice (which printers the default fails)

remedy_direction_flag.status: reporting-only, decidable by inspection, NEVER gated — routes lane-side as a one-line doc fix per Q-0260 (this repo never edits curious-research files)
remedy_direction_flag.finding: line 83 geometry pocket_d = magnet_d - magnet_fit means RAISING magnet_fit makes the pocket SMALLER (tighter) — yet both shipped remedy texts point the dial the other way: the scad comment says "Won't go in? Raise it (e.g. 0.25)" / "Drops out? Lower it", and the README says "won't seat, raise it; if the magnet drops out, lower it" / "raise if too tight". Under the shipped geometry each remedy as written makes its failure WORSE.

drafter_disclosed_reference.note: NON-authoritative drafting-time hand check disclosed in the idea doc; the sim re-derives everything from the pinned constants above and must not trust these — equality is reported, never gated

sensitivity_approx.note: ≈ values only as disclosed; floats, non-authoritative

environment.hermetic: the runner reads only this fixtures.json; zero repo/network reads at verdict time; stdlib only; no wall-clock in any output
